creator: glarkin@FreeBSD.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

On 5/29/11 10:40 AM, Gerald Pfeifer wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/157396; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Gerald Pfeifer <gerald@pfeifer.com>
> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/157396: print/pdftk does not build with lang/gcc46
> Date: Sun, 29 May 2011 16:37:09 +0200 (CEST)
> 
>  Matthias,
>  
>  I believe this is (mostly) a duplicate of PR 157115.  Perhaps you can
>  give the patch in PR 157115 a try?
>  
>  > A: Can pdftk be modified so I can use openjdk? Not sure - if we want
>  >    native code, that might not work.
>  
>  That would be lovely, yes.

As long as gcc can link the main program (a couple of C++ files, IIRC)
with Java libs generated by OpenJDK, perhaps there's a way to make it work.

>  
>  > C: Can GCC46 be modified so that it either makes ends meet without ecj1,
>  >    or pull in the required dependencies?
>  
>  That's what my suggested change to lang/gcc46 tries to accomplish, based
>  on the nice work Greg has done for lang/gcc45.
>  
>  Gerald

I just had a possibly dumb idea to remove the gcj support from gcc, but
still use it build pdftk. Could the pdftk port build an ephemeral
version of gcc with Java enabled, use it to compile pdftk, then remove
it after installing pdftk?  I suppose pdftk would have to be linked
statically to remove shlib dependencies, in that case.  I would not even
want to install that version of gcc+gcj, but just run it directly from
its WRKSRC, if possible.

What do you think?

- -Greg
